title: Reading Index Journey
prompt:
Create a production-ready Reading Index Journey using React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. Build a compact native-scroll reading panel with three labelled chapters and a numbered index that updates as the reader scrolls. The active index marker is teal, while chapter cards remain white on #F4F5F6 with #ECECEE borders, 22px outer corners, soft shadows, #111111 headings, and #4A4A4F body text. Teal is the only bright accent and no card may use a gradient.

Keep scrolling native: never intercept wheel, touch, text selection, or page input. Make the scroll region keyboard focusable with a clear label and 3px dark focus ring. Listen to its scroll position to select the nearest chapter; the index is decorative aria-hidden while a polite live region announces the current chapter only when it changes. Each chapter is a semantic labelled section. Move the teal index marker by 4px with a stiffness 300/damping 26 spring and reveal chapter content with a 220ms opacity and 10px transform transition. Do not add timers.

At 320px, retain the narrow numbered rail and readable copy; in wide detail previews, keep the same two-column rhythm without fixed viewport assumptions. Preserve AA contrast and logical keyboard scrolling. Avoid remote assets, browser storage, random values, network calls, and dynamic code. Honor prefers-reduced-motion by keeping chapters static and moving the marker instantly. Return full TypeScript React client code plus a runnable pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.
